title: Lazy-proof capsule wardrobe
summary: A low-effort men's wardrobe should start with interchangeable neutrals, one accent color, washable layers, and repeatable outfit formulas.
advice: Buy fewer items with high compatibility: jacket, knit, two shirts, two trousers, one versatile shoe, and one weather layer.
Favor machine-washable or easy-care fabrics when the user wants minimal maintenance.
Use one accent family so outfits look intentional without requiring daily decisions.

title: Modern office smart casual
summary: A blazer, structured overshirt, merino layer, tapered trouser, and clean leather sneaker cover most hybrid-office situations.
advice: Choose texture instead of loud patterns for authority without looking overdressed.
A navy or charcoal jacket plus olive, stone, or slate trousers creates easy combinations.
Keep shoes clean and simple; they often decide whether the whole outfit reads polished.

title: Date night without trying too hard
summary: Dark denim or tapered trousers, a soft knit polo, suede jacket, and one quiet accessory produce a confident evening look.
advice: Use darker colors and tactile materials for evening because they photograph well and hide wear.
Avoid novelty pieces; one strong texture is enough.
Fit should be close through the shoulder and taper below the knee.

title: Creative relaxed polish
summary: A chore jacket, camp collar shirt, relaxed taper trouser, and color-block accessory can look expressive while staying easy.
advice: Let one piece carry personality while the rest stays grounded.
Earthy green, rust, indigo, and cream play well together without screaming for attention.
Avoid tight fits when the target vibe is creative and relaxed.

title: Travel and no-fuss care
summary: Wrinkle-resistant knits, stretch wool blends, and dark shoes make a capsule useful for trips and rushed mornings.
advice: Prefer packable layers and fabrics that recover overnight.
Dark, matte shoes survive travel better than pale canvas.
Avoid garments that require dry cleaning unless they unlock many outfits.

title: Fit priorities for men
summary: Shoulders, trouser break, sleeve length, and shoe shape have more impact than brand names.
advice: Jacket shoulders should sit cleanly; sleeve and trouser length can be altered later.
Trousers should taper enough to reveal shoe shape but not pull across the thigh.
Budget for minor tailoring when buying a blazer or wool trouser.
